revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:比売語曽社]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Himekoso Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Higashikunisaki District}} | location = [[:en:Oita Prefecture|Oita Prefecture]] [[Higashikunisaki District]], [[:en:Himeshima, Oita|Himeshima Village]] 5118 | deity = Himekoso-no-Kami | Sacred Object = | Rank = Village Shrine, {{ill|Kokushi-genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi-genzai-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Shakaku|qid=Q10444029}} | Foundation = Unknown | Chief Priest = | Main Hall = | Other names = | Pilgrimage Stamp = | Festival = | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Himeshima|qid=Q1351003|1=Himeshima, Ōita}} }}
'''Himekoso Shrine''' (比売語曽社, ''Himekoso-sha'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located on {{ill|Himeshima|ja|姫島_(大分県)|ceb|Hime_Shima_(pulo_sa_Hapon,_Ōita-ken)|it|Himeshima_(isola)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himeshima|1=Himeshima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3785700}} in [[Higashikunisaki District]], [[:en:Oita Prefecture|Oita Prefecture]]. It is also referred to as '''Himekoso Jinja'''. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Himekoso-no-Kami|ja|比売語曽神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himekoso-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Overview == Himekoso Shrine enshrines Himekoso-no-Kami, the deity from whom Himeshima derives its name. It is classified as a former {{ill|Village shrine (Shinto)|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine (Shinto)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} under the pre-modern {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, and as a {{ill|Kokushi-genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi-genzai-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Shakaku|qid=Q10444029}}. Its founding date is unknown. A grand spring festival is held annually on April 3.
The term ''himekoso'' is an archaic word meaning a shrine dedicated to a "princess" or "lady."
According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, during the reign of Emperor Suinin, a prince from the {{ill|Oka Kingdom|ja|意富加羅国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oka Kingdom|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (believed to be a state in what is now southern Korea), named {{ill|Tsunuga Arashito|ja|都怒我阿羅斯等|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunuga Arashito|qq=}}, sought to marry a maiden ({{ill|Akaruhime-no-Kami|ja|阿加流比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akaruhime-no-Kami|qq=}}) born from a white stone. However, the maiden vanished, and Tsunuga followed her across the sea to Japan, eventually reaching both Settsu and Himeshima, where she became the deity of Himekoso Shrine. The Himekoso Shrine in Settsu is identified as {{ill|Himekoso Shrine|ja|比売許曽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himekoso Shrine (Higashinari Ward, Osaka)|qq=}}, though alternate interpretations exist.
Other theories about Himekoso-no-Kami's identity include: - A daughter of {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|天津彦根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アマツヒコネ|1=Amatsuhikone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10940228}} named {{ill|Himekoso-no-Mikoto|ja|比売許曽命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himekoso-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135578919|1=Himekoso-no-Mikoto}} (also known as Okusaka Ohime-no-Tomeri). - The wife of {{ill|Amenohiboko|ja|天之日矛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hiboko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノヒボコ}}, a prince of Silla. - A consort of King Pasanine from Silla. - {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シタテルヒメ}}, a daughter of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|qid=Q276944|qid=Q276944|lt=Okuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}}. - Tamayorihime, associated with the {{ill|Manano Chōja legend|ja|真名野長者伝説|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manano Chōja legend|1=Manano Chōja legend|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11582906}}. - {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売大神|lt=Hime-no-Ōkami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}}, one of the enshrined deities at {{ill|Usa Jingū|en|Usa Jingū|qid=Q715632|lt=Usa Shrine|1=Usa Jingū}}.
The {{ill|Kameyama Shrine|ja|亀山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kameyama Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Kure, Hiroshima|en|Kure, Hiroshima|qid=Q223439|lt=Kure, Hiroshima|1=Kure, Hiroshima}}, Hiroshima Prefecture, is said to have been relocated from Himeshima in 679 CE and was formerly known as Himeshima Shrine.
== Notes == {{Notes help}} {{Reflist}} <references group="lower-alpha"/>
== External Links == * [http://www.himeshima.jp/bunkazai/sonota/bunkazai4.html Himeshima’s Cultural Assets (Other Cultural Assets)] – Himeshima Village
